Vegetation_Type: Subalpine/montane spruce-dominated conifer forest, Austria (likely SE Alps)
Notes: Limited web documentation. Site code suggests Austrian alps ENF, likely managed Norway spruce. No specific disturbance events found.

Vegetation_Type: Austrian peatland/wetland (likely lowland bog or fen)
Notes: Limited web documentation. WET IGBP classification. No disturbance events retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed forest (Fagus sylvatica / Picea abies), Zöbelboden LTER, Upper Austria, ~900 m elevation
Intensity: Low–partial; physiological stress, not stand-replacing
Notes: Zöbelboden is an Austrian LTER site tracking acid rain recovery since the 1970s. Beech-spruce mixed forest, no stand-replacing mechanical disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Alpine Sphagnum peatland; Bogong High Plains, Victoria; dominated by Sphagnum cristatum, candle heath, rope rush
Notes: Established 2017. Intact ombrotrophic peatland classified as endangered ecological community by Parks Victoria. Snow cover 2–3 months/yr. No logging, fire, or drainage disturbance.

Vegetation_Type: Mulga savanna woodland (Acacia aneura); average canopy height 6.5 m; Pine Hill cattle station, NT
Intensity: Low–moderate; episodic dieback
Notes: Located on working cattle station ~200 km N of Alice Springs. Mean annual precip ~300 mm, highly variable (100–750 mm range). No stand-replacing fire or logging. Significant inter-annual variability from drought; Mulga can experience episodic die-off after extreme droughts. Station established 2010.

Vegetation_Type: Mallee eucalyptus woodland (Eucalyptus dumosa/incrassata/oleosa/socialis) with spinifex; Calperum, South Australia
Intensity: Low–moderate; spinifex and leaf litter burned, epicormic resprouting; not stand-replacing
Notes: Fire is natural and essential disturbance in Mallee ecosystems. Jan 2014 fire burned spinifex ground cover and canopy leaf/branch material; trees resprout vigorously. Site established ~2010. Highly variable rainfall 100–500 mm.

Vegetation_Type: Savanna woodland, Fletcherview station, Queensland; likely Eucalyptus- or Acacia-dominated open woodland
Notes: Limited documentation retrieved. WSA savanna, typical of NE Queensland dry tropics. Fires are likely recurrent in surrounding landscape but no site-specific event records found.

Vegetation_Type: Tropical savanna; Eucalyptus miniata / E. tetrodonta co-dominant overstory; 14–16 m canopy; Black Jungle Conservation Reserve, Darwin NT
Intensity: Low–moderate (dry-season surface fire, grass-layer combustion); stand persists
Notes: Primary research focus is fire impacts on carbon, water, and energy fluxes. Howard Springs has a fire return interval of 1–3 years; fire is the principal mechanism of carbon loss in this tropical savanna. Tower established 2001.

Vegetation_Type: Scots pine (Pinus sylvestris) plantation on former heathland; ~2 ha research zone in mixed coniferous/deciduous forest; Brasschaat, 20 km NE of Antwerp
Intensity: Low–moderate; physiological stress, reduced NEE
Notes: First-generation Scots pine plantation established 1929 on former heathland (Campine plain). Active ICOS Class 1 station since 2020. Drought 2018 caused significant flux anomalies documented in published data. No stand-replacing logging or fire.

Vegetation_Type: Short-rotation coppice (SRC) poplar plantation; 11 ha; 12 genotypes; Lochristi (~11 km from Ghent), E. Flanders
Disturbance_Type: Coppice harvest (stand-replacing for the cut cohort)
Intensity: Stand-replacing within coppice rotation (~2 yr)
Notes: Planted April 2010 on agricultural land. 8,000 trees/ha. Harvested to stump Feb 2012 (2-yr rotation); subsequent regrowth and second cut 2014. Each harvest removes entire above-ground biomass; belowground persists. No fertilization or irrigation.

Vegetation_Type: Heathland (dwarf shrubs — Calluna vulgaris, Erica spp.); Hoge Kempen National Park, Maasmechelen
Notes: Europe's first heathland ICOS station. Hoge Kempen NP; heathland is managed by occasional mowing/grazing to maintain open heath structure. No stand-replacing disturbance documented. ICOS Class 2 candidate.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed forest: beech (Fagus sylvatica), Douglas fir (Pseudotsuga menziesii), Norway spruce, Scots pine; Vielsalm, Ardennes, Belgium
Intensity: Partial; selective thinning removes sub-dominant stems
Notes: Oldest Belgian flux tower, established 1996. ICOS ecosystem station. Thinning every 6 years maintains stand structure. No windthrow or bark-beetle stand-replacing event documented. Tower replaced from 40 m scaffolding to 52 m pylon in 2009.

Vegetation_Type: Tropical evergreen broadleaf forest (EBF); likely lowland Amazon terra firme
Notes: Brazilian EBF site; site name "ITA" potentially refers to Itajaí/Itacoatiara or similar Amazonian region. No specific disturbance event documented in available sources. Site likely represents intact or near-intact primary tropical forest.
Source: FLUXNET metadata; no web source found

Vegetation_Type: Northern peatland bog; Attawapiskat River, Ontario; Boreal Zone
Notes: Pristine northern bog established as part of Ontario Ministry of Environment & Climate Change Far North carbon flux monitoring network. Remote boreal peatland, no logging or fire.

Vegetation_Type: Northern peatland fen; Attawapiskat River, Ontario; Boreal Zone
Notes: Companion fen site to CA-ARB. Pristine northern fen; Far North monitoring network. No disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Canadian peatland (likely black spruce/Sphagnum bog or fen)
Notes: Limited web documentation. WET IGBP. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Burns Bog secondary measurement site; Delta, BC; raised ombrotrophic bog
Intensity: Partial; peat was harvested in portions of the bog complex; main bog core remains largely intact
Notes: Burns Bog (Delta Burns Bog) is one of the largest raised bogs in North America. Peripheral areas have history of peat extraction and fire; the flux tower area is within conservation zone. Rewetting ongoing.

Vegetation_Type: Burns Bog primary measurement site; Delta, BC; raised ombrotrophic bog
Intensity: Partial; core bog protected; surrounding peat farm areas degraded
Notes: Burns Bog had extensive peat harvesting on ~2,100 ha in the 20th century; conservation purchase 2004; active rewetting of cut-over areas. Tower site within the conservation zone monitoring carbon exchange.

Vegetation_Type: Canadian northern peatland (likely black spruce WET site, Alberta or BC)
Notes: Limited documentation. WET classification. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Canadian northern peatland (likely bog or fen, Saskatchewan/Manitoba region)
Notes: Limited documentation. WET classification. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland (OSH); likely subarctic/boreal shrub tundra transition or regenerating post-fire shrubland
Notes: Limited documentation. OSH IGBP. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland (OSH); companion site to CA-LU1
Notes: Limited documentation. OSH IGBP. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Ombrotrophic Sphagnum bog (raised bog); Mer Bleue, Ottawa; ~28 km²
Notes: Longest continuous EC measurement record in Northern peatlands (since May 1998). Protected wetland at eastern edge of Ottawa. Pristine ombrotrophic bog; no drainage, logging, or fire. Monitored as part of FLUXNET-Canada and McGill-led research.
Source: Mer Bleue LPVE Supersite; AmeriFlux CA-Mer

Vegetation_Type: Canadian peatland (likely bog or fen, provincial peatland complex)
Notes: Limited documentation. WET classification. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Canadian peatland companion site to CA-PB1
Notes: Limited documentation. WET classification. No disturbance info retrieved.

Vegetation_Type: Eastern white pine (Pinus strobus) plantation; Turkey Point, Ontario
Intensity: Stand-replacing (entire old-field converted to planted forest)
Notes: Established 2002 on former agricultural field. Part of Turkey Point Research Forest chronosequence. Managed plantation; no subsequent logging.

Vegetation_Type: Eastern white pine plantation; Turkey Point, Ontario
Disturbance_Type: Plantation establishment on former agricultural land
Intensity: Stand-replacing (afforestation)
Notes: Planted 1974, mid-rotation plantation. Part of Turkey Point chronosequence. Managed; no subsequent logging disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Naturally regenerated deciduous broadleaf forest (~90 yr old); Turkey Point, Ontario; sugar maple/beech/oak
Intensity: Partial; selective thinning recorded
Notes: 90-yr-old naturally regenerated DBF; some past management thinning mentioned in site documentation. No stand-replacing event.

Vegetation_Type: Subalpine Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); elevation ~1639 m, Davos, Switzerland
Intensity: Partial — stand partially damaged; tower site itself not fully reset
Notes: Large-scale windstorm Vivian (Feb 1990) caused widespread blowdown in surrounding Swiss Alps; the CH-Dav tower footprint was partially affected; site has been operated continuously since 1997 by WSL/ETH Zurich; drought stress documented 2003 and 2018
Source: Zielis et al. (2014) Biogeosciences; Merbold et al. various; AmeriFlux site: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CH-Dav

Vegetation_Type: Mixed deciduous-coniferous forest (beech, oak, Norway spruce, silver fir); Laegern hill, Switzerland
Notes: Long-term site operated by ETH Zurich since 2004; no major stand-replacing disturbance documented; managed as protected forest reserve; some selective harvesting historically prior to reserve status

Vegetation_Type: Peatland / Sphagnum-dominated bog; Patagonian region, Los Lagos, Chile
Notes: Relatively pristine ombrotrophic peatland (turbera) in Chilean Lake District; limited published disturbance history; site is in a remote, lightly managed area; some regional grazing pressure but no major documented disturbance
Source: Pancotto et al. and Chilean flux network publications; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CL-SDP

Vegetation_Type: Mixed deciduous broadleaf and conifer plantation; Beijing outskirts, China
Intensity: Stand-replacing (afforestation on previously degraded/agricultural land)
Notes: Part of Beijing afforestation program; young plantation forest established on formerly degraded land; some thinning management; urban proximity means air pollution stress documented
Source: Liu et al. various; FLUXNET China publications; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CN-BeO

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf shrub/woodland; Helan Mountain foothills, Inner Mongolia, China
Intensity: Low–partial
Notes: Semi-arid site in transition zone; historical overgrazing and land degradation; seasonal drought stress is primary stressor; enclosure/restoration management applied

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous needle-leaf forest (larch, Larix principis-rupprechtii); Saihanba, Hebei, China
Intensity: Stand-replacing (afforestation on former degraded grassland/desert)
Notes: Saihanba Mechanical Forest Farm — one of China's largest afforestation projects initiated in 1962 on formerly bare, wind-eroded sandy land; managed larch plantation; thinning operations documented
Source: Guo et al. various; FLUXNET China; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CN-Sb1

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest (Pinus sylvestris var. mongolica); Saihanba, Hebei, China
Intensity: Stand-replacing (afforestation on former degraded sandy land)
Notes: Co-located with CN-Sb1 in Saihanba Forest Farm; Scots pine plantation established in 1960s afforestation campaign; managed plantation with documented thinning
Source: Guo et al. various; FLUXNET China; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CN-Sb2

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland; semi-arid shrub steppe, Nei Mongol / Inner Mongolia, China
Intensity: Low–partial
Notes: Semi-arid shrubland; primary stressors are drought and historical livestock overgrazing; gradual degradation; grazing exclusion/restoration management applied at some sites in this region

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland / semi-arid steppe; Yanchi, Ningxia, China
Intensity: Low–partial
Notes: Semi-arid shrubland in the Loess Plateau transition zone; historically subjected to overgrazing and wind erosion; restoration and grazing exclusion measures in place

Vegetation_Type: Wetland / marsh; Zhangye, Heihe River Basin, Gansu, China
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Inland freshwater marsh in arid inland basin; primary stressor is upstream water diversion reducing inflow to Heihe wetlands; some drainage and agricultural conversion in surrounding area
Source: Li et al. Heihe Basin studies; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CN-Zha

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Beskydy Mts., Czech Republic
Intensity: Stand-replacing (severe, widespread salvage logging across Beskydy region; tower footprint significantly impacted)
Notes: Major bark beetle epidemic triggered by drought (2018) and preceded by windstorm disturbance; widespread mortality and salvage logging in the Beskydy Mountains documented extensively; one of the most severe European bark beetle outbreaks on record in this region; tower still operating but ecosystem shifted to post-disturbance recovery
Source: Lindner et al.; Hlásny et al. (2019) Forest Ecology & Management; Senf & Seidl (2021); ICOS site; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CZ-BK1

Vegetation_Type: Floodplain forest / hardwood lowland deciduous forest (oak, ash, hornbeam); Lanžhot, South Moravia, Czech Republic
Intensity: Low–partial
Notes: Floodplain forest with regular spring inundation regime; managed floodplain; drought stress increasing post-2015; no major stand-replacing disturbance documented; some selective logging
Source: Acosta et al. (2013) Biogeosciences; ICOS; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CZ-Lnz

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Rájec-Němčice, Moravian Highlands, Czech Republic
Intensity: Stand-replacing (large areas of spruce mortality; salvage logging)
Notes: Part of the broader Central European bark beetle epidemic; severe drought in 2018 weakened spruce stands allowing Ips typographus outbreak; extensive salvage logging; site in post-disturbance recovery
Source: Hlásny et al. (2019); ICOS CZ; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CZ-RAJ

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest (oak, hornbeam, beech); Štítná nad Vláří, White Carpathians, Czech Republic
Notes: Mature semi-natural deciduous forest in White Carpathians Protected Landscape Area; no major disturbance documented; some management thinning historically

Vegetation_Type: Fen / wetland (Phragmites australis-dominated); Třeboň Basin, Czech Republic
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Located in Třeboň Biosphere Reserve; historic drainage for agricultural use; rewetting restoration efforts since early 2000s; managed as nature reserve; no fire or logging disturbance
Source: Dušek et al. (2009) Wetlands; ICOS CZ; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/CZ-wet

Vegetation_Type: Fen / degraded peatland (Phragmites, Carex); Ahlenmoor, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Germany
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Drained and degraded fen; rewetting restoration ongoing as part of German peatland restoration; no fire; ongoing paludiculture/rewetting research site
Source: Holl et al.; German peatland flux network; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/DE-Akm

Vegetation_Type: European beech forest (Fagus sylvatica); Hainich National Park, Thuringia, Germany
Disturbance_Type: None documented (prior military use as buffer zone)
Disturbance_Year: Pre-1990 land use change (military exclusion zone pre-1990, then national park from 1997)
Intensity: Low (historical removal of management)
Notes: Hainich is notable for having been a military exclusion zone (Sperrgebiet) during German division (pre-1990), which incidentally preserved the old-growth beech forest; no recent fire, logging, or bark beetle disturbance; became UNESCO World Heritage beech forest 2011
Source: Knohl et al. (2003) Agric. For. Meteorol.; Kutsch et al.; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/DE-Hai

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce / mixed conifer forest; Black Forest / Hartheim region, Baden-Württemberg, Germany
Notes: Managed conifer forest in Black Forest; some routine forest management thinning; no major disturbance event documented at the tower scale

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest (oak-dominated); Hordorf/Hohes Holz, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany
Notes: Lowland oak-dominated forest in sub-continental climate; no major disturbance documented; some management thinning; drought stress 2018–2019 noted in regional context

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce / mixed conifer; Mangfallgebirge / pre-Alpine Bavaria, Germany
Notes: Pre-Alpine conifer forest; managed forest; no major documented disturbance at tower scale; note: site may overlap with or be near areas affected by windstorm Kyrill (2007) but no confirmed direct impact

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce forest; Erzgebirge (Ore Mountains), Saxony, Germany
Intensity: Stand-replacing (widespread spruce mortality and salvage logging across Erzgebirge)
Notes: Erzgebirge experienced severe bark beetle (Ips typographus) outbreak following 2018 drought; widespread Norway spruce mortality; salvage logging; part of the pan-Central-European bark beetle epidemic; tower footprint impacted
Source: Senf & Seidl (2021) Nature Communications; Hlásny et al. (2019); AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/DE-Obe

Vegetation_Type: Shrubland / heathland (Calluna vulgaris-dominated); Rollesbroich catchment, Eifel, Germany
Intensity: Low (managed)
Notes: Calluna heathland managed by periodic cutting and/or prescribed burning to maintain open heathland structure; no uncontrolled wildfire; management burns are routine conservation practice
Source: Hoeltl et al.; TERENO Eifel; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/DE-RuC

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Tharandt, Saxony, Germany
Intensity: Partial (Kyrill: partial windthrow gaps); Stand-replacing in surroundings (2018–2020 bark beetle)
Notes: Windstorm Kyrill (Jan 2007) caused partial blowdown; subsequent bark beetle outbreak; regional bark beetle epidemic 2018–2020 caused widespread spruce mortality in Tharandt Forest; one of the longest-running European flux sites (since 1996); post-disturbance dynamics well studied
Source: Grünwald & Bernhofer (2008) Tellus; Feigenwinter et al.; Senf & Seidl (2021); AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/DE-Tha

Vegetation_Type: European beech forest (Fagus sylvatica); Sorø, Zealand, Denmark
Notes: Mature managed beech forest; long-term ICOS/CarboEurope site since 1996; routine silvicultural thinning; no major stand-replacing disturbance; storm damage minor (1999 storm caused some gaps in region but site not severely impacted); drought stress 2018 documented
Source: Pilegaard et al. (2003) J. Geophys. Res.; Granier et al.; ICOS DK; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/DK-Sor

Vegetation_Type: Hemi-boreal / boreonemoral Scots pine forest (Pinus sylvestris); Soontaga, Estonia
Notes: Scots pine-dominated hemi-boreal forest on nutrient-poor sandy soil; managed but no major disturbance documented; Estonian flux site; some selective logging historically; no major windthrow or bark beetle event confirmed at tower scale
Source: Varlagin et al.; ICOS EE; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/EE-Stg

Vegetation_Type: Savanna / open woodland (dehesa); holm oak (Quercus ilex) with grass understorey; Albuera, Extremadura, Spain
Intensity: Low (managed)
Notes: Typical Spanish dehesa — open oak savanna with continuous cattle grazing; no stand-replacing fire documented at this site; recurrent summer drought; tower operated ~2015–2020
Source: Lopes et al. various; FLUXNET Spain; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/ES-Abr

Vegetation_Type: Closed shrubland (matorral); Mediterranean scrub dominated by Rosmarinus, Cistus, Pistacia; Caroig Massif, Valencia, Spain
Notes: Semi-arid Mediterranean shrubland on calcareous substrate; no major fire or logging disturbance documented at tower scale; some historical land abandonment leading to shrub encroachment; site active 2021–2024

Vegetation_Type: Freshwater marsh / Mediterranean wetland; Phragmites australis and Juncus dominated; Doñana region, Huelva, Spain
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Located near Doñana Biosphere Reserve; wetland subject to seasonal flooding variability; surrounding area has history of drainage for agriculture; severe 2004–2005 drought affected Doñana wetlands; ongoing hydrological management; trait extraction required 3 km buffer (wetland masked in standard trait maps)

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest (post-fire pine regeneration); Pinus halepensis; Hellín, Murcia, Spain
Intensity: Stand-replacing
Notes: Site name "Hellín Burnt" explicitly indicates post-fire origin; Pinus halepensis reforestation / natural regeneration following wildfire; tower established to study post-fire carbon dynamics; paired with ES-Hen (unburned control); active 2019–2024
Source: Bonet et al.; ICOS Spain; FLUXNET; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/ES-HeB

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest (mature Pinus halepensis plantation / natural forest); Hellín, Murcia, Spain
Notes: Unburned mature Aleppo pine forest; paired control site for ES-HeB (post-fire site) ~600 m away; represents undisturbed reference vegetation; active 2019–2024
Source: Bonet et al.; ICOS Spain; FLUXNET; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/ES-Hen

Vegetation_Type: Savanna / dehesa; holm oak (Quercus ilex) + grass; Majadas del Tietar North, Cáceres, Spain
Intensity: Low
Notes: Majadas del Tietar experimental dehesa complex; ES-LM1 and ES-LM2 are manipulative treatment plots (different grazing or nitrogen treatments); long-term carbon flux study by MPI-BGC and CSIC; ongoing grazing management; no fire
Source: Migliavacca et al.; El-Madany et al. (2018) JGR-Biogeosciences; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/ES-LM1

Vegetation_Type: Savanna / dehesa; holm oak (Quercus ilex) + grass; Majadas del Tietar South, Cáceres, Spain
Intensity: Low
Notes: Paired with ES-LM1; part of Majadas del Tietar dehesa manipulation experiment; different grazing/nutrient treatment from LM1; no fire

Vegetation_Type: Savanna / dehesa; holm oak (Quercus ilex) + grass; Las Majadas de Tietar, Cáceres, Spain
Intensity: Low
Notes: The original "Las Majadas" tower (precursor/parallel to ES-LM1/LM2); continuous cattle grazing; no fire or logging; drought stress documented 2017 (extreme drought); MPI-BGC operated
Source: Migliavacca et al. (2017) Nature Plants; El-Madany et al. various; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/ES-LMa

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen broadleaf forest (citrus/orange orchard); Moncada, Valencia, Spain
Intensity: Low (managed agro-ecosystem)
Notes: Managed citrus orchard; IGBP EBF reflects evergreen canopy; not a natural forest — subject to annual management (pruning, fertilization, irrigation, harvest); no fire or major disturbance; active 2018–2024

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland (matorral); Mediterranean scrub (Halimium, Cistus, Lavandula); Monte Blanco, Huelva, Spain
Notes: Open shrubland on sandy acid soils; proximity to Doñana region; no major fire event documented at tower scale; possible historical burning in surrounding landscape; site active 2021–2024

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest; mixed deciduous woodland (Quercus faginea, Q. pubescens); Tarazona de la Mancha, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain
Notes: Semi-arid deciduous oak woodland; no major disturbance documented; some historical land use (agriculture/grazing) prior to forest recovery; drought stress in semi-arid continental climate; active 2020–2024

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest; Pinus sylvestris plantation; Yeste, Albacete, Spain
Notes: Managed Scots pine plantation in Sierra de Alcaraz; no major fire documented at this specific tower; possible wildfire history in surrounding region (Sierra de Segura/Alcaraz is a fire-prone landscape); some thinning management; active 2019–2024

Vegetation_Type: Boreal Scots pine forest (Pinus sylvestris); SMEAR II station; Hyytiälä, Pirkanmaa, Finland
Intensity: Stand-replacing (historical)
Notes: The current stand was established in 1962 by planting after clear-cutting; mature managed Scots pine; one of the most intensively studied boreal forests globally; no fire or bark beetle outbreak documented since establishment; routine thinning management; ongoing since 1996
Source: Hari & Kulmala (2005) Boreal Env. Res.; Kolari et al. (2009) Tellus; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FI-Hyy

Vegetation_Type: Boreal/sub-arctic Norway spruce forest (Picea abies) near treeline; Kenttärova, Lapland, Finland
Notes: Remote old-growth-like Norway spruce forest near the northern treeline; no logging or fire disturbance documented; very low human pressure; active 2018–2024
Source: Aurela et al. various; FMI Finland; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FI-Ken

Vegetation_Type: Boreal Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Lettosuo, Salo region, Finland
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Drained paludified (formerly waterlogged) spruce forest; original site had been drained for forestry; site transitioned from drained spruce to study carbon balance of managed boreal forest; some selective logging historically; active 2009–2024

Vegetation_Type: Boreal Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Ranskalankorpi, Häme-Tavastia, Finland
Notes: Managed boreal spruce forest; relatively recently established tower (2020–2023); no major disturbance documented at tower scale; part of Finnish flux network (ICOS Finland)
Source: Finnish Meteorological Institute / Univ. Helsinki; ICOS FI; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FI-Rk2

Vegetation_Type: Boreal ombrotrophic peatland / raised bog (Sphagnum fuscum, Eriophorum); Siikaneva, Tampere region, Finland
Notes: Natural raised bog; pristine ombrotrophic peatland; adjacent to FI-Hyy; no drainage, fire, or logging disturbance documented; intensive long-term peatland carbon flux measurements; active ~2005–present
Source: Rinne et al. (2007) Glob. Change Biol.; Aurela et al. various; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FI-Sii

Vegetation_Type: Boreal/sub-arctic Scots pine forest (Pinus sylvestris); Värriö, Lapland, Finland
Notes: Remote sub-arctic pine forest in Värriö Strict Nature Reserve; very low human impact; no logging or fire documented; long-term background monitoring site; active 2016–2024
Source: Hari et al.; University of Helsinki Värriö research station; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FI-Var

Vegetation_Type: Maritime pine forest (Pinus pinaster); Bilos, Landes de Gascogne, France
Intensity: Stand-replacing (large fire affecting Landes forest)
Notes: The Landes de Gascogne maritime pine forests suffered catastrophic wildfires in summer 2022 (Gironde/Landes fires were among France's largest in decades, ~74,000 ha burned); the FR-Bil site and surrounding Landes forest were significantly impacted; active 2014–2024; the tower likely registered fire-driven carbon flux changes post-2022
Source: Berbigier et al.; Ogée et al.; Landmann et al. (2022) ICOS France; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FR-Bil

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest (mixed Pinus halepensis and Quercus ilex); Font-Blanche, Bouches-du-Rhône, France
Notes: Mediterranean mixed forest on calcareous soils; high wildfire risk landscape (Provence); no confirmed stand-replacing fire at the tower footprint; drought stress documented; active 2008–2024
Source: Simioni et al.; Limousin et al.; ICOS France; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FR-FBn

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest (Quercus petraea and Q. robur); Fontainebleau-Barbeau, Seine-et-Marne, France
Notes: Mature temperate sessile/pedunculate oak forest; managed as part of Fontainebleau Forest (ONF); selective logging and some thinning historically; no stand-replacing disturbance; 1999 storm caused minor blowdown in region; drought stress 2003 and 2018–2019 documented; active 2005–2024
Source: Granier et al. (2008) Agric. For. Meteorol.; Delpierre et al.; ICOS France; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FR-Fon

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen broadleaf forest (Quercus ilex / holm oak); Puéchabon, Hérault, France
Notes: Mature holm oak forest on calcareous substrate; no logging or fire documented at tower scale; drought-adapted xeric forest; 2003 heat wave / drought caused some leaf damage but no stand-level mortality; one of the longest-running Mediterranean flux sites (since 1999/2000); active continuously since 2000
Source: Rambal et al. (2003) Glob. Change Biol.; Reichstein et al. (2003); ICOS France; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/FR-Pue

Vegetation_Type: Low Arctic open shrubland / dwarf shrub heath (Betula nana, Salix, Dryas); Disko Island, West Greenland
Notes: Arctic shrub tundra on Disko Island; no fire, logging, or grazing disturbance documented; climate warming-driven shrub encroachment (shrubification) is the primary vegetation change agent; permafrost present; active 2020–2024
Source: Groendahl et al.; Arctic station Qeqertarsuaq (Disko Island), Univ. Copenhagen; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/GL-Dsk

Vegetation_Type: Semi-arid evergreen needle-leaf forest; Pinus halepensis plantation; Yatir Forest, Negev fringe, Israel
Intensity: Stand-replacing (historical — afforestation on degraded semi-arid scrubland)
Notes: Yatir Forest is one of the world's largest semi-arid planted forests, established by the JNF (Jewish National Fund) beginning in the early 1960s on bare/degraded calcareous soils at the edge of the Negev Desert; ~3000 ha; no subsequent fire or logging disturbance; drought stress is the dominant ecological constraint; active since 2000; Pinus halepensis; elevation ~652 m
Source: Rotenberg & Yakir (2010) Science; Grünzweig et al. various; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/IL-Yat

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland / Mediterranean macchia; Arzelle, Lake Garda area, Brescia, Italy
Notes: Mediterranean/sub-Mediterranean shrubland near Lake Garda; thermophilous shrub community; no major fire or logging disturbance documented; active 2016–2024; ICOS Italy affiliate

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest; Bosco Fontana nature reserve (Quercus robur, Fraxinus, Carpinus); Mantua, Italy
Notes: Bosco Fontana is a small (233 ha) national nature reserve with old-growth lowland oak forest; protected since 1949; no logging since reserve establishment; no fire documented; active 2019–2024
Source: Spano et al.; ICOS IT; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/IT-BFt

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest (mixed broadleaf; beech, oak, chestnut); Bussoleno, Susa Valley, Piedmont, Italy
Notes: Mixed deciduous montane forest in the Western Alps foothills; no major disturbance documented at tower scale; some historical logging and chestnut coppicing in the region; active 2021–2024

Vegetation_Type: Mixed forest / evergreen Mediterranean woodland (Quercus ilex, Pinus pinea, Pinus pinaster); Castelporziano Presidential Estate, Rome, Italy
Notes: Large protected estate forest south of Rome; managed as a presidential reserve (Tenuta di Castelporziano); no logging or fire since reserve establishment; some management of exotic species; drought stress documented in Mediterranean climate; active since ~2012; IGBP listed as MF (mixed forest) but sometimes EBF in other classifications
Source: Sabbatini et al.; ICOS IT; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/IT-Cp2

Vegetation_Type: Subalpine Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Lavarone plateau, Trentino, Italy
Notes: Managed subalpine spruce forest; some selective harvesting historically; no major windthrow or bark beetle outbreak documented at the tower; active 2003–2020; elevation ~1353 m; part of CarboEurope/ICOS network
Source: Marcolla et al. (2003) Agric. For. Meteorol.; Cescatti & Zorer (2003); AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/IT-Lav

Vegetation_Type: Open shrubland / Mediterranean macchia; Lison-Pramaggiore, Treviso/Venice area, Italy
Disturbance_Type: Hydrological management (agricultural drainage of surrounding area)
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Low-elevation shrubland/marginal vegetation near Venice lagoon hinterland; surrounding landscape is intensively drained agricultural plain; site may experience altered hydrology from land drainage; no fire or direct logging disturbance documented; active 2016–2024

Vegetation_Type: Subalpine Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Renon / Ritten plateau, South Tyrol, Italy
Notes: Well-studied subalpine spruce forest in the eastern Italian Alps; managed forest with some selective harvesting; no stand-replacing windthrow or bark beetle outbreak documented at the tower level (note: 2018–2019 bark beetle outbreaks occurred in surrounding South Tyrol but direct tower impact not confirmed); active 1999–2024; elevation ~1687 m
Source: Cescatti & Marcolla (2004) Ann. For. Sci.; ICOS IT; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/IT-Ren

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest (Pinus pinea / stone pine); San Rossore, Pisa, Tuscany, Italy
Notes: Coastal stone pine forest (Pineta) in Migliarino-San Rossore-Massaciuccoli Regional Park; protected park forest; no logging or fire documented at the tower since park establishment; active since ~2013; elevation ~4 m (coastal flatlands)
Source: Tedeschi et al.; ICOS IT; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/IT-SR2

Vegetation_Type: Cool-temperate deciduous broadleaf forest; mixed broadleaf (Quercus mongolica, Betula, Acer); Appi Highland, Iwate Prefecture, Japan
Notes: Cool-temperate deciduous forest in mountainous Iwate; long-term FFPRI (Forestry and Forest Products Research Institute) site; no major fire, logging, or windthrow documented at tower scale; active 2000–2022
Source: Hirano et al.; Ohta et al. various; FFPRI Japan; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/JP-Api

Vegetation_Type: Cool-temperate deciduous broadleaf forest; mixed broadleaf (Quercus serrata, Carpinus, Magnolia); Yamashiro, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
Notes: Warm-temperate/cool-temperate transition zone forest; FFPRI Yamashiro research forest; managed but no major stand-replacing disturbance documented; active 2000–2023; elevation ~320 m
Source: Hirano et al.; FFPRI Japan; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/JP-Yms

Vegetation_Type: Subtropical evergreen broadleaf forest (Castanopsis sieboldii, Machilus, Ficus); Yona, Okinawa Island, Japan
Notes: Subtropical evergreen broadleaf (lucidophyllous) forest on Okinawa; FFPRI research site; relatively undisturbed subtropical forest; typhoon disturbance is the primary natural stressor (Okinawa is frequently hit by typhoons); no major logged or burned area documented at tower; active 2013–2022
Source: FFPRI Japan; Hirano et al.; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/JP-Ynf

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest; Korean pine / mixed conifer (Pinus koraiensis, Chamaecyparis); Gwangneung Experimental Forest, Gyeonggi-do, South Korea
Notes: Gwangneung National Forest is one of Korea's oldest experimental forests; protected since 1468 (Joseon Dynasty); minimal disturbance for centuries; no logging or fire; conifer plot; active 2016–2020; part of KoFlux network
Source: Kim et al. (2006); Kwon et al.; KoFlux; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/KR-Kw1

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest; mixed temperate deciduous (Quercus mongolica, Carpinus, Acer); Gwangneung Experimental Forest, Gyeonggi-do, South Korea
Notes: Deciduous plot in Gwangneung National Forest; protected for centuries; no logging or fire; ~300–500 m from KR-Kw1 conifer plot; active 2016–2020; part of KoFlux network
Source: Kim et al. (2006); Kwon et al.; KoFlux; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/KR-Kw2

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needle-leaf forest; Scots pine plantation (Pinus sylvestris); Loobos, Veluwe, Gelderland, Netherlands
Intensity: Stand-replacing (historical)
Notes: Loobos is a ~60–70-year-old Scots pine plantation established on former heathland (open sand) on the Veluwe sandy plateau; 1999 windstorm caused minor gap creation in surrounding Veluwe forests but no major damage confirmed at tower; drought stress documented; no fire; active since 1996; one of the longest-running European flux sites
Source: Dolman et al. (2002) Tellus; Jacobs et al.; ICOS NL; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/NL-Loo

Vegetation_Type: Arctic/subarctic tundra wetland / waterlogged tundra (moss-sedge-shrub); Chersky, northeastern Siberia, Russia
Intensity: Partial
Notes: Northeastern Siberian tundra/wetland complex near the Northeast Science Station (Chersky); primary stressor is permafrost degradation and active layer deepening driven by climate warming; some thermokarst development; no fire, logging, or grazing documented; active 2014–2021
Source: Corradi et al.; Zimov et al.; FLUXNET Russia; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/RU-Ch2

Vegetation_Type: Boreal Norway spruce forest — dry variant (Picea abies); Fyodorovskoye, Tver Oblast, Russia
Notes: Boreal spruce forest in central European Russia; paired with RU-Fyo; "dry spruce" stand variant; some historical logging in surrounding landscape but no major disturbance at tower footprint documented; active 2015–2024
Source: Milyukova et al.; Vygodskaya et al.; FLUXNET Russia; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/RU-Fy2

Vegetation_Type: Boreal bog / peatland (Sphagnum-dominated raised bog); Fyodorovskoye, Tver Oblast, Russia
Notes: Boreal raised Sphagnum bog in Tver Oblast; co-located with RU-Fyo/RU-Fy2 complex; natural ombrotrophic peatland; no drainage or fire documented; active 2015–2020
Source: Milyukova et al.; FLUXNET Russia; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/RU-Fy4

Vegetation_Type: Boreal Norway spruce forest (Picea abies); Fyodorovskoye, Tver Oblast, Russia
Notes: The primary Fyodorovskoye boreal spruce tower; one of the longest-running Russian flux sites (since 1998); old-growth-like boreal spruce on typical central-Russian upland; no fire or logging documented at tower scale; some windthrow gaps historically; active 1998–2024
Source: Milyukova et al. (2003) Tellus; Vygodskaya et al.; FLUXNET Russia; AmeriFlux: https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/sites/siteinfo/RU-Fyo

Vegetation_Type: Oligotrophic boreal mire (Sphagnum-dominated); interconnected bog/fen complex with glacial till ridges; Degero, N Sweden ~70 km W of Gulf of Bothnia
Notes: Scientific measurements since 1909; eddy covariance since 1995; ICOS station since 2013. Pristine boreal mire, no drainage, fire, or cutting disturbance. Part of boreal mire complex methane flux network.

Vegetation_Type: Swedish boreal peatland/mire (fen or bog)
Notes: Limited documentation. WET IGBP. Likely part of the Vindeln/Svartberget research catchment area mire network in N Sweden. No disturbance records found.

Vegetation_Type: Swedish boreal peatland/mire
Notes: Limited documentation. WET IGBP. Part of N Sweden boreal mire network. No disturbance records found.

Vegetation_Type: Managed Norway spruce (Picea abies) plantation (>97% spruce); southern Sweden; maritime temperate climate
Notes: Hyltemossa ICOS station, southern Sweden. Managed monoculture Norway spruce. Bark beetle research study conducted here in summer 2019 (measuring BVOC emissions from infested trees), but this was a study — the site itself does not have a documented large-scale outbreak or logging event. Regular forestry management expected but no stand-replacing event documented.
Source: ICOS SE-Htm; Biogeosciences 2023 bark beetle study

Vegetation_Type: Norway spruce / Scots pine boreal forest; 100–130-yr-old stand; southern boreal zone ~30 km N of Uppsala
Intensity: Stand-replacing: 30.5 ha (~14,000 trees) removed; understory also cleared
Notes: Norunda ICOS station, established 1994. A major clear-cut removed all 100–130-yr Norway spruce and Scots pine in a 300 m radius of the main tower in autumn 2022. Site replanted with Scots pine (~2,500 plants/ha) in spring 2024. Pre-logging data: 1994–2022; post-clear-cut data ongoing.

Vegetation_Type: Managed boreal Norway spruce / Scots pine forest; Rosinedal, N Sweden; Vindeln area
Notes: ICOS Sweden site; part of Svartberget Experimental Forest area, Vindeln. Managed boreal forest; no specific stand-replacing disturbance documented. ICOS Level 2 data available 2014–2018.

Vegetation_Type: Swedish boreal peatland/mire
Notes: Limited documentation. WET IGBP. N Sweden boreal mire area. No disturbance records found.

Vegetation_Type: Boreal mixed Norway spruce / Scots pine forest; Svartberget Experimental Forest, NW of Umeå, N Sweden
Notes: Research station since 1923; ICOS Class 2 station since 2011. Experimental forest with research clear-cuts and thinning plots elsewhere on the 1,076 ha property, but the main flux tower footprint has not experienced documented stand-replacing disturbance. Fire research conducted at nearby sites.

Vegetation_Type: Ombrotrophic peatland (Auchencorth Moss); ~17 km SW of Edinburgh, Scotland
Intensity: Partial: drainage reduced peat water table; now progressively rewetting as drains become ineffective
Notes: Peatland drained >100 years ago; field drains progressively blocked/vegetated over time leading to slow rewetting of the site. Seasonal sheep grazing (low intensity, ongoing). Peat extraction in peripheral areas. Flux measurements since 1994/1995; ICOS ecosystem station since 2017. No modern fire or logging.

Vegetation_Type: Northern hardwood forest; Bartlett NH; sugar maple/beech/yellow birch
Notes: White Mountain National Forest site. No major fire, logging, or insect outbreak documented at tower site.

Vegetation_Type: Thermokarst collapse scar boreal bog; Bonanza Creek, Tanana Lowlands, interior Alaska; ~1 m peat; intermediate-to-old age
Intensity: Low–moderate; thermokarst collapse feature, peat development; not acute stand-replacing event
Notes: Measures C/H2O/CH4 fluxes across permafrost thaw gradient. Site 2011–present.

Vegetation_Type: Old thermokarst collapse scar boreal bog; Bonanza Creek, interior Alaska; several hundred years old
Intensity: Historical stand-replacing thermokarst; now stable bog
Notes: Older thermokarst feature than US-BZB. Site 2018–present.

Vegetation_Type: Woody savanna; Bears Ears National Monument, Cedar Mesa, Utah; pinyon-juniper woodland
Intensity: Low; occasional spring cattle grazing
Notes: Semiarid steppe; drought responses monitored. Site 2019–present.

Vegetation_Type: Riparian mesquite (Prosopis velutina) woodland; San Pedro River, SE Arizona; 60% canopy cover, groundwater-dependent
Notes: Riparian DBF sustained by alluvial aquifer; evapotranspiration significantly exceeds precipitation. Site 2000–2021.

Vegetation_Type: Sparse canopy ENF; likely Oregon/Pacific NW (forest_mean≈0 at all buffer sizes)
Notes: **Still excluded from RF benchmark** (mortality_intensity_pct all NA; forest_mean≈0). Very sparse or patchy canopy; DBF/ENF classification may not reflect current canopy conditions.

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needleleaf forest; Central Oregon
Notes: No disturbance info on AmeriFlux page or via search.

Vegetation_Type: Restored tidal freshwater wetland; Gilbert Tract, Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta, CA
Intensity: Stand-replacing (wetland): prior upland/agriculture converted to tidal wetland
Notes: DWR restoration project. Tidal action restored October 2021; flux tower data from 2021–present. Pre-restoration was non-tidal.

Vegetation_Type: Restored tidal salt pond wetland; Eden Landing Ecological Reserve, San Francisco Bay; 6,400 acres diked marsh + 10-yr-old restored salt ponds
Intensity: Stand-replacing (wetland): former salt production ponds converted to tidal marsh; restoration ongoing
Notes: CA Dept. of Fish & Wildlife site. Salt ponds restored to tidal marsh over ~10 years; flux measurements 2018–present.

Vegetation_Type: Ridge and slough marsh; Everglades, Florida; permanent wetland
Notes: Everglades peat/marl forming wetland; no disturbance on record. Site 2007–present.

Vegetation_Type: Shrub tundra with permafrost thaw; Healy, Alaska; permafrost degrading
Intensity: Partial: progressive thermokarst/permafrost degradation; shifting vegetation
Notes: Arctic permafrost-thaw gradient site. Site 2008–present.

Vegetation_Type: Subalpine spruce-fir forest; GLEES, Wyoming; trees >400 yr old
Intensity: Stand-replacing: ~85% of forested basal area killed; overstory mortality
Notes: Engelmann spruce/subalpine fir. Decade-long beetle outbreak culminating ~2008; 85% basal area mortality. Historical grazing (closed early 1990s).

Vegetation_Type: Tidal salt marsh; North Inlet, Crab Haul Creek, SC; ~40% tall Spartina, 40% short Spartina, 20% creek
Notes: Pristine tidal marsh. Site 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Mature longleaf pine (Pinus palustris) forest; Hobcaw Barony, SC
Intensity: Low: periodic prescribed burning to maintain longleaf ecosystem structure
Notes: Research focus is role of fire in longleaf pine C/water cycling. Site 2018–present.

Vegetation_Type: Young longleaf pine restoration plantation; Hobcaw Barony, SC
Intensity: Stand-replacing: original mixed forest clearcut, herbicided, replanted with containerized longleaf pine seedlings
Notes: Longleaf pine restoration project; site monitors C/water balance during restoration. Data 2019–present.

Vegetation_Type: Impounded brackish wetland; Minim Creek, SC; historic rice field adjacent to tidal creek; managed for waterfowl
Intensity: Partial: water levels managed for habitat; rice-field origin
Notes: Historic rice field converted to managed waterfowl impoundment. Site 2020–present.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed DBF; Pellston, Michigan; northern mixed hardwoods
Notes: No disturbance on record.

Vegetation_Type: Tidal marsh; Hill Slough, Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta, CA; pickleweed and wetland vegetation
Intensity: Stand-replacing (wetland): 649 acres breached; converted from diked marsh to active tidal marsh
Notes: Ducks Unlimited restoration. Water now 0.5–1 m above soil at high tide. Site monitoring since 2021.

Vegetation_Type: Arctic tundra heath shrubland; Imnavait Creek, Kuparuk Basin, AK
Notes: Arctic OSH; Ridge Station established 2007. Part of Arctic Observatory Networks.

Vegetation_Type: Arctic wet sedge tundra fen; Imnavait Creek, AK
Notes: Fen station established 2007. Parallel water track hydrology typical of arctic hillslopes.

Vegetation_Type: Arctic tundra (companion to US-ICh); Imnavait Creek, AK
Notes: Established 2007 as part of AON/SEARCH program.

Vegetation_Type: Chihuahuan Desert shrubland (creosote + honey mesquite); Jornada Experimental Range, NM
Intensity: Stand-replacing: grassland converted to shrubland by overgrazing + shrub encroachment
Notes: Jornada Range Reserve est. 1912 to investigate grazing-induced degradation. Now creosote-mesquite desert shrub; occasional herbivore visits but no current overgrazing. Site 2010–present.

Vegetation_Type: Boreal fen; Lily Lake, Kenai Peninsula, AK
Notes: Subarctic fen. Data 2021–present.

Vegetation_Type: Boreal fen; Marcell Experimental Forest, Minnesota
Notes: EC tower since 2007; CH4 since 2009. No drainage or disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Ponderosa pine plantation; Metolius, Oregon; post-fire managed stand
Intensity: Stand-replacing: entire original forest killed by fire, salvage logged, replanted 1990
Notes: Metolius cluster site examining successional stages. Data 2010–present.

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest (oak-hickory); Ozark, Missouri
Intensity: Partial; episodic mortality, no stand-replacing event
Notes: Documented drought-related tree mortality at site. DBF, long-term monitoring.

Vegetation_Type: Pinyon-juniper woodland; Mountainair, NM; ~60% tree cover (Pinus edulis + Juniperus monosperma)
Intensity: Partial–significant: piñon pine mortality July 2013 continuing through 2015; juniper unaffected
Notes: Combined drought (2011–2013) and Pinon ips bark beetle drove piñon die-off. Site well-documented for EFP changes post-mortality.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed evergreen conifer forest; Mt Bigelow, Catalina Mts, AZ; secondary forest
Intensity: Historical: forest re-established post-disturbance ~1930s; no recent event
Notes: Secondary forest growing since ~1930s. 30-m tower installed 2002; data 2009–present.

Vegetation_Type: Restored tidal freshwater wetland; Mayberry, Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta, CA; cattail/tule dominant
Intensity: Stand-replacing: prior upland (pepperweed + grassland) converted to 300-acre wetland by flooding
Notes: DWR/Ducks Unlimited project. Tower installed October 2010. Tidal flooding since 2010.

Vegetation_Type: Loblolly pine (Pinus taeda) plantation; Dismal Swamp, NC; coastal plain
Intensity: Partial: drought-induced stress + commercial thinning to reduce competition
Notes: Managed plantation; thinning operations 2007 and 2009. Site 2005–present.

Vegetation_Type: Loblolly pine plantation; companion to US-NC2; post-harvest regeneration
Intensity: Stand-replacing: complete harvest followed by replanting
Notes: Site documented post-harvest carbon dynamics. Data 2005–present.

Vegetation_Type: Black spruce (Picea mariana) boreal forest; Poker Flat Research Range, Fairbanks, AK
Notes: Subarctic ENF; data 2010–present. No disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Low sagebrush (Artemisia arbuscula) closed shrubland; Reynolds Creek Experimental Watershed, ID; BLM managed
Notes: Semiarid rangeland. Data 2014–present.

Vegetation_Type: Mountain big sagebrush (Artemisia tridentata) closed shrubland; Reynolds Creek, ID; BLM managed
Notes: Companion site to US-Rls. Data 2014–present.

Vegetation_Type: Early-successional deciduous broadleaf forest; post-fire Alaskan interior; Poker Flat, Fairbanks, AK
Intensity: Stand-replacing: black spruce forest burned, replaced by successional deciduous broadleaf vegetation
Notes: Site established 2008 specifically to monitor post-fire forest recovery dynamics in Alaskan boreal. DBF classification reflects deciduous early-successional vegetation (birch/alder) post-fire.

Vegetation_Type: Mountain big sagebrush shrubland; Reynolds Creek watershed, ID
Intensity: Partial: low-intensity prescribed burn; sagebrush reduced, recovery ongoing at time of measurement
Notes: Research examines post-fire sagebrush shrubland recovery dynamics. Data 2014–present (7 yr post-fire).

Vegetation_Type: Wyoming big sagebrush (Artemisia tridentata wyomingensis) open shrubland; Reynolds Creek, ID
Notes: BLM managed shrubland. Data 2012–present.

Vegetation_Type: Chihuahuan Desert shrubland (Larrea/creosote + perennial C4 grasses); Sevilleta NWR, NM
Intensity: Partial (remote): prior livestock grazing; grazing ended 1973, effects now considered negligible
Notes: No modern disturbance. Sevilleta NWR, McKenzie Flats ungrazed since 1973. Data 2007–present.

Vegetation_Type: Evergreen needleleaf forest; Sagehen Creek, Sierra Nevada, CA
Notes: No disturbance info on AmeriFlux page.

Vegetation_Type: Naturally regenerated Florida longleaf / slash pine flatwoods; Alachua County, FL
Notes: Naturally regenerated pine flatwoods. No disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Velvet mesquite (Prosopis velutina) woody savanna; Santa Rita, Arizona
Intensity: Partial–gradual: mesquite invasion of former semidesert grassland
Notes: Primary research focus is how mesquite encroachment alters C/water cycling. No fire or logging. Data 2004–present.

Vegetation_Type: Tidal salt marsh; St Jones Reserve, Delaware NERR
Intensity: Partial: long-term management notes "restoring natural vegetation" as goal
Notes: Delaware National Estuarine Research Reserve. Data 2015–2020.

Vegetation_Type: Arctic shrub tundra; Toolik Field Station, AK; closed shrublands
Intensity: Low–artificial: experimental snow addition/redistribution alters snow depth; not stand-replacing
Notes: Research on snow-shrub feedbacks in Arctic tundra. Data 2018–present.

Vegetation_Type: Blue oak savanna (Quercus douglasii); Tonzi Ranch, Sierra Nevada foothills, CA
Notes: Oak savanna; data 2001–present. No disturbance documented.

Vegetation_Type: Restored freshwater wetland (cattail/tule); Twitchell Island West Pond, Sacramento Delta, CA
Intensity: Stand-replacing (wetland): prior upland permanently flooded to ~25 cm depth
Notes: Managed restoration: tower installed 2012, 15 yr post-restoration; cattail/tule established within 3 growing seasons.

Vegetation_Type: Restored freshwater wetland (cattail/tule); Twitchell East End, Sacramento Delta, CA
Intensity: Stand-replacing: prior cornfield flooded; wetland plants introduced
Notes: Former cornfield; Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta drainage history (since 1850s). Tower installed Nov 2013; flooding commenced Jan 2014.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed deciduous broadleaf forest (aspen/birch/maple); UMBS, Michigan
Intensity: Partial: ~35% LAI removed by girdling of aspen/birch; accelerated succession
Notes: FASET (Forest Accelerated Succession ExperimenT) girdled trembling aspen and paper birch to simulate successional transition and study effects on C/water fluxes. Tower site within girdled area.

Vegetation_Type: Ponderosa pine / mixed conifer subalpine forest; Valles Caldera NM, Jemez Mtns, NM
Intensity: Stand-replacing: every single tree killed; surface organic matter burned off
Notes: Major wildfire May 2013 killed 100% of trees; subsequent research used rapid-response tower at nearby unburned site. Data gap May 2013–Jan 2014 during infrastructure restoration. Data 2007–present (with gap).

Vegetation_Type: Ponderosa pine (Pinus ponderosa) forest with Gambel oak understory; Valles Caldera NM, Jemez Mtns, NM
Notes: Semiarid ponderosa pine at 2,501 m. Research focus on water/carbon sensitivity to drought/precipitation. Data 2007–present.

Vegetation_Type: Juniper (J. monosperma) savanna with C4 perennial grasses; Willard, central NM
Intensity: Low: intermittent livestock grazing
Notes: Semiarid savanna; primary driver is precipitation variability. Data 2007–present.

Vegetation_Type: Young industrial timber production ENF; Yacolt Burn State Forest, SW Washington; ~40 yr old second-growth
Disturbance_Type: Historical stand-replacing fire ("Yacolt Burn") + industrial logging
Intensity: Stand-replacing (historical): 1902 Yacolt Burn destroyed original forest; now managed industrial forest
Notes: Named for 1902 Yacolt Burn (~238,000 acres). Now replanted timber production forest. Typical rotation management expected. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest; Blandy Experimental Farm, Boyce, VA; mixed use rural-suburban landscape
Notes: NEON site in rapidly urbanizing megapolitan area. Data 2016–present.

Vegetation_Type: Black spruce boreal ENF; Caribou Creek–Poker Flats Watershed, AK; Fairbanks area
Notes: NEON Boreal Alaska site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Northern hardwood deciduous forest; Bartlett Experimental Forest, NH
Disturbance_Type: Multiple historical disturbances (logging + fire + disease + storms)
Intensity: Historical: ~70% uncut since early 1900s; 30% managed; beech bark disease partial; ice storm partial
Notes: Historical logging from colonial times through early 1900s. Late 19th c. fire. Beech bark disease from 1940s. Hurricane wind damage 1938 and 1954. Ice storm 1998. Now old secondary forest. NEON site. Data 2016–present.

Vegetation_Type: Black spruce boreal ENF; Delta Junction, AK; interior Alaskan boreal
Notes: NEON Boreal Alaska site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed forest (bottomland hardwood + evergreen); Dead Lake, near Demopolis, AL; Black Warrior River floodplain
Notes: NEON site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest; Great Smoky Mountains NP, TN/NC
Notes: **Still excluded from RF benchmark** (forest_mean=0 despite DBF label; suggests sparse/open canopy or model misclassification). NEON site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Arctic/subarctic shrub tundra; Healy, AK
Notes: NEON Healy site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Longleaf pine forest; Jones Ecological Research Center, Newton, GA
Intensity: Low: dormant-season prescribed fires; natural longleaf ecosystem management
Notes: Managed with low-intensity prescribed burns for conservation of longleaf pine ecosystem. No stand-replacing event. NEON site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Chihuahuan Desert open shrubland; Jornada LTER, NM; creosote/mesquite shrubland
Intensity: Partial: historical grassland degraded to shrubland by livestock grazing since 1800s; current grazing minimal
Notes: Historical overgrazing drove grassland→shrubland transition. Jornada Range Reserve research since 1912. NEON site. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Colorado Plateau open shrubland; Moab, Utah; two zones: grazed shrubland (W) + eastern mesas with evergreen forest
Intensity: Low: active livestock grazing in western half
Notes: NEON site. Dust transport research. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Appalachian deciduous broadleaf forest; Mountain Lake Biological Station, Giles Co., VA; 1,160 m elevation
Notes: NEON site; University of Virginia. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Subalpine conifer forest (Engelmann spruce/subalpine fir); Niwot Ridge NEON, CO; 3,513 m elevation
Notes: NEON alpine site. Air pollution research. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Subalpine ENF; Rocky Mountain National Park, CO; mid-elevation east side Continental Divide
Notes: NEON Rocky Mountain NP site. Chemical pollution/dust deposition research. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Deciduous broadleaf forest; Walker Branch Watershed, Oak Ridge NL, TN
Notes: NEON site in long-term research watershed. Data 2017–present.

Vegetation_Type: Florida longleaf pine flatwoods; Ordway-Swisher Biological Station, FL; open ENF structure
Intensity: Low–moderate: prescribed burns maintain open longleaf pine ecosystem; not stand-replacing
Notes: 9,300-acre station managed with 3–4-yr fire cycle. NEON site. Data 2016–present.

Vegetation_Type: Mixed deciduous broadleaf forest (oak/hickory/ash); Smithsonian Conservation Biology Institute, VA
